mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% represents an extremely accommodative monetary stance. This ultra-low rate environment reduces the cost of capital for equity valuations and increases the present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 137,802 thousand persons shows robust labor market strength. Sustained employment growth signals consumer income stability and aggregate demand resilience, which translates into positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) during periods of positive PAYEMS momentum, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 974 thousand units combined with retail sales at 485,545 million dollars indicate strong consumer demand. When both housing and retail demand accelerate simultaneously, this signals robust economic momentum that supports equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 97.46 (index 2017=100) is near pre-crisis trend levels. A decline in INDPRO below 96.5 signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 96.5 or declines > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO triggers the threshold condition, or Rank IC > -0.02 during supply weakness periods.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 11.1% is elevated relative to historical norms. When unemployment rises above 10.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 10.5% or increases > 0.3 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) when UNRATE exceeds 10.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 257.214 combined with ultra-low FEDFUNDS at 0.08% creates a large negative real interest rate environment. This regime incentivizes equity allocation over bonds, but only when inflation expectations remain anchored. If CPI accelerates above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%, equity returns turn negative over 60 days due to stagflation concerns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of stagflation-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 at 18,977.363 billion chained 2017 dollars shows solid economic expansion. When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) during periods of above-2.5% GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales momentum at 485,545 million dollars combined with housing starts at 974 thousand units and payroll employment at 137,802 thousand reveals synchronized strength across consumer spending, housing investment, and labor income. This three-way confirmation of demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days, but only when all three series show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: All three series (RSAFS, HOUST, PAYEMS) show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when all three demand indicators show concurrent growth, or the three-way interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.
